name: Conrad Herwig
abstract: Lee Conrad Herwig III (born November 1, 1959) is an American jazz trombonist from New York City.

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival: Tito Puente's Golden Latin Jazz All-Stars/ Eddie Palmieri and His Afro-Cuban Jazz Octet
Blue Valley West High School Wind Symphony, Jazz Combo, and Chorale
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

works.t: If you could see me now
Lady in the Dark: Act II. Final Scene: My Ship
The Wizard of Oz: Over the Rainbow

concerts.label: Rutgers University Mason Gross School of the Arts Faculty Chamber Concert

works.t: Danzas Argentinas, Op. 2: 2. Danza de la moza donosa
Improvisation No. 15 in C Minor (Hommage à Edith Piaf)

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: I had Mintzer on the clarinet and tenor, of course Conrad, trombone. That’s real three-horn writing, a lot of writing on that record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Davis
about_quotes.evidence: people like Steve Turré, Robin Eubanks, Conrad Herwig, Birch Johnson, Jim Pugh played on that
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Davis interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, April 1, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Eddie Palmieri
about_quotes.evidence: we had there Brian Lynch, Conrad Herwig, and Donald Harrison... It came up from an idea from Conrad Herwig, the trombonist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Eddie Palmieri — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Michael Abene
about_quotes.evidence: You know Conrad Herwig, the trombone player, lives ten minutes from me, I never see him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Michael Abene interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Powell
about_quotes.evidence: some of Conrad Herwig’s albums were recorded by this man, my Japanese friend.
about_quotes.doc_title: Benny Powell interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 16, 1999 [interview 2 of 2]
